evals.name: new-cloudflare-worker-with-kv
evals.prompt: I want to deploy a Cloudflare Worker that uses a KV namespace for caching. Set up Alchemy in an empty directory called my-worker. Use Bun. I'll use a CF API token I'll add later.
evals.expected_output: Scaffolded project: package.json with alchemy dep + scripts, alchemy.run.ts that awaits a Worker with a KV binding, src/worker.ts hello-world, .env.example listing required vars, .gitignore with .env/.alchemy/.wrangler entries, ALCHEMY_PASSWORD generated into .env, no secrets committed or inlined.

evals.name: add-to-existing-nextjs
evals.prompt: I have a Next.js 15 app at the repo root and I want to switch from Vercel to Cloudflare via Alchemy. Wire it up so I can deploy with `bun deploy`. I want a KV binding called CACHE.
evals.expected_output: Installs alchemy + @opennextjs/cloudflare, writes alchemy.run.ts with Nextjs() resource and KV binding, adds open-next.config.ts, updates package.json scripts (deploy/destroy/dev), updates .gitignore, generates ALCHEMY_PASSWORD, does NOT overwrite existing next.config.js values, warns about vercel.json if present.

evals.name: aws-lambda-ddb-strict-security
evals.prompt: Set up Alchemy to manage an AWS Lambda function backed by a DynamoDB table. I will use AWS SSO via my local profile 'dev-acct'. Make sure no AWS keys end up in the repo. The app name is order-service.
evals.expected_output: alchemy.run.ts with Role + Table + Function resources, uses environment variables sourced from AWS profile (no inline keys), .env.example mentions AWS_PROFILE=dev-acct only, .gitignore covers secrets, ALCHEMY_PASSWORD generated, README or printed instructions explain `aws sso login --profile dev-acct` before deploy, refuses to write any AWS credential into any committed file.
